<!-- directives: 307 across 9 topic files -->

# Governance Directives

Follow these directives in every file you write or edit.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

These are invariants. Violation is never acceptable.

- Every edikt tier-1 command MUST be a `.md` file, and every tier-1 template MUST be `.md`, `.yaml`, or `.sh`. No TypeScript, no compiled binaries, no build step in tier-1. (ref: INV-001)
- The tier-1 system MUST work by copying files into `~/.claude/commands/`. (ref: INV-001)
- `.sh` MUST only be used for hook templates under `templates/hooks/` — those scripts MUST be POSIX bash with stdlib-only Python heredocs (no third-party runtime deps). (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 files MUST NOT runtime-depend on tier-2 binaries. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 MUST NOT have a `package.json`, root-level `go.mod`, or build files at the tier-1 root. (ref: INV-001)
- All tier-1 commands MUST live in `commands/` as `.md` files. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 templates MUST be plain `.md`, `.yaml`, or (under `templates/hooks/` only) `.sh` files in `templates/`. `.sh` hook templates MUST be POSIX bash and MUST NOT depend on third-party runtimes — embedded Python heredocs are stdlib-only. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-2 code MUST live under `tools/<name>/` with its own `go.mod`; tier-2 binaries MUST NOT be committed to the repo. (ref: INV-001)
- Architecture Decision Records MUST be immutable once accepted. (ref: INV-002)
- Once the status is changed to `accepted`, its content — context, decision, and consequences — MUST never be edited again. (ref: INV-002)
- The only permitted mutation after acceptance is updating the `Status:` field to `Superseded by ADR-NNN` or `Deprecated`. (ref: INV-002)
- When a decision changes, a new ADR MUST be written that supersedes the old one. (ref: INV-002)
- The old ADR MUST be updated to `Status: Superseded by ADR-NNN` and left otherwise intact. (ref: INV-002)
- When asked to update, fix, or improve an accepted ADR: stop and create a new ADR instead. (ref: INV-002)
- The new ADR must reference the superseded one (`Supersedes: ADR-NNN`). (ref: INV-002)
- The old ADR status line must be updated to `Superseded by ADR-NNN`. (ref: INV-002)
- No other changes to the accepted ADR are permitted. (ref: INV-002)
- Every hook that emits JSON MUST construct that JSON via a structured serializer with untrusted values passed as data (not concatenated into a string). (ref: INV-003)
- Building JSON by shell string concatenation is NEVER permitted in hooks. (ref: INV-003)
- Pre-merge CI lint MUST grep for echo or printf JSON shell-concatenation patterns in templates/hooks/*.sh and install.sh and MUST return zero matches. (ref: INV-003)
- Unit-test fixtures per hook MUST assert output is `json.loads`-parseable when inputs contain embedded `"`, `\`, and newline characters. (ref: INV-003)
- `/edikt:sdlc:audit` and `/edikt:sdlc:review` MUST read INV-003 before reviewing any change under `templates/hooks/`. (ref: INV-003)
- Hooks MUST NOT emit a `systemMessage`, `additionalContext`, or other Claude-facing channel whose body contains a shell command interpolating subagent-derived, file-content-derived, git-identity-derived, or otherwise attacker-influenceable text. (ref: INV-004)
- Hooks that need to record state (event logs, gate firings, override decisions) MUST perform that recording themselves from within the hook process. (ref: INV-004)
- Claude MUST receive only static instructions plus structured data. (ref: INV-004)
- CI lint MUST reject any hook whose output contains the regex `(systemMessage|additionalContext)[^}]*\$\{` OR whose output contains a markdown code fence labelled `bash` inside a hook-protocol field. (ref: INV-004)
- Security regression tests under `test/security/hooks/` MUST include at least one test per hook that attempts to inject `'; rm /tmp/sentinel; '` into an agent-derived field and asserts the file `/tmp/sentinel` is never removed. (ref: INV-004)
- Every edikt-managed region in a user file has a cryptographic integrity mechanism that MUST be verified before the region is overwritten. (ref: INV-005)
- Managed markdown regions MUST be validated by byte-range overlap of the resolved file — NEVER by regex over `old_string` or `new_string`. (ref: INV-005)
- The install/upgrade writer MUST verify the live JSON's managed-key hash against the sidecar before overwriting managed keys. (ref: INV-005)
- An edit whose resolved byte range (markdown) or managed-key hash (JSON) would mutate a managed region MUST be blocked unless issued by an explicitly allowlisted edikt operation (compile, install, upgrade, migration). (ref: INV-005)
- The pre-tool-use hook MUST execute the byte-range guard on every Edit/Write under governance-watched paths. (ref: INV-005)
- Every managed sentinel region MUST carry a hash anchor after the first compile following v0.5.0 upgrade; missing anchor on an active block MUST be seeded by compile. (ref: INV-005)
- Unit tests at `test/security/sentinel/` MUST cover the overlap matrix (adjacent, inside, outside, cross-boundary, CRLF, Unicode) plus the hash mismatch and bootstrap paths. (ref: INV-005)
- `~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json` is required for any edikt-managed `settings.json`; its absence MUST trigger the interactive permissions prompt on upgrade. (ref: INV-005)
- Any value that originates outside edikt's immediate control — filenames discovered via filesystem scan, configuration values read from `.edikt/config.yaml`, environment variables, CLI flags (`--ref`, `--model`, etc.), URL refs, and hook payload fields — MUST be validated against an allowlist regex before it is interpolated into a shell argv element, a URL, a prompt string, or a file path. (ref: INV-006)
- Validators MUST normalize with NFKC + casefold + whitespace-strip so that Unicode lookalikes and trailing whitespace cannot bypass allowlist comparisons. (ref: INV-006)
- Attacker-influenceable values that flow into argv MUST be passed as separate argv elements, never interpolated into a quoted string that is then evaluated. (ref: INV-006)
- Sandboxes created by the test harness or benchmark runner to execute Claude sessions MUST be hermetic. (ref: INV-007)
- Test and benchmark sandboxes MUST NOT copy the host user's `~/.claude/settings.json`, user-global settings, hooks, shell rc files, SSH keys, or any other host credential or configuration into the sandbox. (ref: INV-007)
- The `setting_sources` list passed to subprocess Claude invocations in tests MUST be restricted to `["project"]`; `"user"` is forbidden. (ref: INV-007)
- The sandbox MUST receive only a curated minimal `settings.json` written by the test harness. (ref: INV-007)
- Security regression tests under `test/security/sandbox/` MUST assert that, for a representative benchmark run, the sandbox `settings.json` contains no `hooks` key and no entries outside the curated allowlist. (ref: INV-007)
- CI grep: `grep -rn 'setting_sources=\[.*user' test/` MUST return zero matches. (ref: INV-007)
- `test/integration/benchmarks/results/claude-*/` MUST be `.gitignore`d; committed result directories (summaries/baselines) MUST be grepped for credential patterns pre-merge. (ref: INV-007)
- Every user-facing install, upgrade, or documentation URL that resolves to a mutable source — raw.githubusercontent.com, releases/latest/download/, branch refs, or any path a repo owner can silently update — MUST NOT be used. (ref: INV-008)
- All user-facing URLs MUST pin to a specific release tag (releases/download/vX.Y.Z/... or an equivalent immutable ref). (ref: INV-008)
- Pre-release CI check MUST grep README.md docs/ .github/workflows/ and return zero matches for raw.githubusercontent.com/.+/main/ or releases/latest/download/ before a release tag is cut. (ref: INV-008)
- Security regression test under test/security/release/ MUST assert no branch-tracking URL is present in user-facing files. (ref: INV-008)
- Release runbook MUST include a step to update the pinned version in README and re-run the grep. (ref: INV-008)

## Reminders

Before acting, check the relevant constraint.

[edikt:reminders:start]: #
- Before modifying `/edikt:upgrade` behavior → verify it never overwrites a file marked with `<!-- edikt:custom -->` or listed in `agents.custom` (ref: ADR-005)
- Before adding a new template → verify `.edikt/templates/` lookup order has project override taking precedence over edikt default (ref: ADR-005)
- Before modifying rule install logic → verify extension files are concatenated after the built-in pack and both are version-controlled (ref: ADR-005)
- Before modifying templates/agents/evaluator.md or templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md → verify BLOCKED is declared as a valid per-criterion verdict and valid overall verdict (ref: ADR-010)
- Before adding a new evaluator-invocation site → verify it routes through plan.md's orchestrator or explicitly includes the BLOCKED-verdict contract (ref: ADR-010)
- Before modifying the evaluator flow in commands/sdlc/plan.md → verify headless-first attempt, fallback to subagent with visible warning banner, and BLOCKED verdict parsing are all present (ref: ADR-010)
- Before modifying test/run.sh → verify EDIKT_SKIP_HOOK_TESTS is not set as a required env var (gate must remain opt-out) (ref: ADR-011)
- Before removing a fixture pair → verify a _note and target_phase are added to fixtures.yaml §9.1 (ref: ADR-011)
- Before modifying _runner.sh → verify run_staged_fixture is absent; it belongs only in _staged_runner.sh (ref: ADR-011)
- Before modifying test/integration/conftest.py auth gate → verify pytest_collection_finish is used (not pytest_sessionstart) and the error message lists all three remediation paths: claude auth login, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and SKIP_INTEGRATION=1 (ref: ADR-012)
- Before adding new tests under test/integration/ → verify regression museum tests in test/integration/regression/ do not require any claude auth and the gate does not fire for them (ref: ADR-012)
- Before updating CI workflow auth configuration → verify ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is documented as the headless fallback only, not the primary mechanism (ref: ADR-012)
- Before modifying the release workflow → verify SHA256SUMS is generated by sha256sum and uploaded as a release asset alongside both tarballs (ref: ADR-013)
- Before modifying bin/edikt install → verify it fetches SHA256SUMS and calls verify_against_sidecar() before extracting the payload tarball (ref: ADR-013)
- Before migrating a hook to JSON → verify the user-visible message is preserved byte-for-byte inside the JSON wrapping (ref: ADR-014)
- Before re-adding a removed fixture pair → run the hook against the payload and capture actual output as the expected file — never hand-author expected outputs (ref: ADR-014)
- Before making content changes to hook messages → ensure it is a separate commit with a separate fixture update, not folded into the wrapping migration (ref: ADR-014)
- Before modifying install.sh → verify it contains no package-manager invocations and references only tier-1 artifacts (ref: ADR-015)
- Before adding a command file with package-dependent helpers → verify it declares `tier: 2` in frontmatter (ref: ADR-015)
- Before releasing a tier-2 install → verify test/integration/test_install_tier2.py::test_tier1_checksums_unchanged passes (ref: ADR-015)
- Before modifying .github/workflows/release.yml → verify it publishes edikt-payload-<tag>.tar.gz, SHA256SUMS, and SHA256SUMS.sig.bundle as Release assets (ref: ADR-016)
- Before modifying install.sh or bin/edikt install/upgrade → verify cosign verify-blob with certificate-identity-regexp is called before extracting any artifact (ref: ADR-016)
- Before updating the README install command → verify URL pins to releases/download/v<PINNED>/install.sh and grep returns zero matches for raw.githubusercontent.com/.*/main/ (ref: ADR-016)
- Before modifying templates/settings.json.tmpl permissions block → verify deny list includes all destructive Bash patterns, destructive git patterns, plaintext HTTP fetches, and sensitive file reads enumerated in ADR-017 (ref: ADR-017)
- Before running bin/edikt upgrade on a customized settings.json → verify the user is prompted before the managed permissions block is overwritten (ref: ADR-017)
- Before shipping a new edikt install → verify ~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json is created and records the canonical hash of the managed permissions keys (ref: ADR-017)
- Before modifying evaluator agent templates → verify they emit a JSON object conforming to `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json`, not prose verdicts (ref: ADR-018)
- Before marking a plan phase done → verify every shell-command criterion has `evidence_type: "test_run"` or the harness forces BLOCKED (ref: ADR-018)
- Before running upgrade on a pre-0.5.0 project → verify `bin/edikt upgrade` writes stub verdicts with `meta.grandfathered: true` for all `done` phases (ref: ADR-018)
- Before modifying any hook under templates/hooks/ → verify the hook is one of the four listed in ADR-019 before bundling content change with JSON-wrapping migration (ref: ADR-019)
- Before regenerating fixtures for the four listed hooks → verify fixtures are generated by running the updated hook against its payload, not hand-authored (ref: ADR-019)
- Before merging a hook change → verify git log -p templates/hooks/*.sh shows non-listed hooks have wrap-only diffs with no content changes (ref: ADR-019)
- Before modifying commands/gov/compile.md → verify it still delegates YAML parse, hash computation, and template rendering to the tier-2 helper and has not re-inlined those steps (ref: ADR-020)
- Before modifying tools/edikt/compile.py → verify byte-equal output test passes: two consecutive compiles of the same source produce byte-equal .claude/rules/ trees via diff -r in CI (ref: ADR-020)
- Before releasing a compile change → verify no-op recompile exits in under 500 ms with zero LLM calls and full regenerate completes in under 5 s wall-clock (ref: ADR-020)
- Before adding a new tier-2 helper → verify it is a Go module under `tools/<name>/` with `go.mod` and `go.sum` committed (ref: ADR-021)
- Before modifying the release workflow → verify all four platform targets are cross-compiled and each binary's SHA-256 is added to `SHA256SUMS` (ref: ADR-021)
- Before changing `install.sh` or `bin/edikt` → verify they remain POSIX bash with no Go binary dependency at install time (ref: ADR-021)
- Before migrating a subcommand from edikt-shell to Go → verify EDIKT_SHELL_CALLER=1 delegation still works for all remaining unmigrated subcommands (ref: ADR-022)
- Before any release build → verify tools/edikt/.gitignore excludes the compiled binary and it is absent from the git tree (ref: ADR-022)
- Before any user-facing error message or help text change → verify the word edikt-shell does not appear in any user-visible output (ref: ADR-022)
- Before modifying evaluator agent templates → verify no literal verdict path strings exist in templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md and templates/agents/evaluator.md (ref: ADR-025)
- Before writing verdict storage logic → verify the path resolves through paths.plans config value and contains no hardcoded literal (ref: ADR-025)
- Before running bin/edikt upgrade on a pre-0.5.0 project → verify grandfathered verdicts are written under the resolved paths.plans value, not a hardcoded docs/product/plans/verdicts path (ref: ADR-025)
- Before modifying `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` → verify no keyword-grep agent identity block exists; only `evaluator_output.agent` and canonical payload fields set AGENT_NAME (ref: ADR-026)
- Before modifying `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` → verify `AGENT_IDENTITY_SOURCE` can only be `evaluator_output` or `payload`; the legacy value `grep-fallback` must not exist in the code (ref: ADR-026)
- Before releasing v0.6.0 → verify `test/unit/hooks/test_subagent_stop.sh` includes `subagent-stop-no-agent.json` fixture and that expected output is `{"continue": true}` (ref: ADR-026)
- Before verifying the sidecar schema → check `templates/schemas/sidecar.schema.json` exists, validates v1 shape, and forbids `source_hash` / `agent_prompt_version` / `directives_hash` at root via `additionalProperties: false` (ref: ADR-027)
- Before running `gov:compile` → verify ADR-008 has `status: superseded`, `superseded_by: ADR-027`, and `**Status:** Superseded by ADR-027` as its top body line (ref: ADR-027)
- Before running `gov:compile` on a pre-migration project → verify compile exits non-zero with a single-line actionable error directing the user to `edikt migrate sidecars` (ref: ADR-027)
- Before modifying Phase B merge code → verify the static-analysis test in test/integration/governance/ confirms no Agent/Task/subprocess symbol is transitively reachable from the merge path (ref: ADR-028)
- Before invoking gov:compile --check in CI → verify it exits 1 on stale sidecars without dispatching any subagent (ref: ADR-028)
- Before updating Phase A concurrency or failure handling → verify subagent failures still log to .edikt/state/compile-errors.log and Phase B is gated on Phase A success (ref: ADR-028)
- Before invoking a new `bin/edikt` subcommand from tier-1 markdown → verify the verb is in ADR-029 Rule 3's enumerated list (ref: ADR-029)
- Before adding a tier-1 command that calls a tier-2 binary → verify it declares `tier_2_dependency:` and `on_absent:` in its frontmatter (ref: ADR-029)
- Before merging a tier-1 command that invokes tier-2 → verify absence-detection emits an actionable error citing the helper by name (ref: ADR-029)
- Before invoking a new `bin/edikt sidecar` subcommand from tier-1 markdown → verify `sidecar` is in ADR-029 Rule 3's permitted verb list (ref: ADR-031)
- Before adding a new `/edikt:adr:enrich` tier-2 dependency → verify frontmatter declares `tier_2_dependency: edikt` and `on_absent: refuse-and-direct-user` (ref: ADR-031)
- Before modifying the sidecar schema → verify `prohibitions[]` remains a separate top-level array in `templates/schemas/sidecar.v1.schema.json` (ref: ADR-032)
- Before amending ADR-032 → verify compile UX feedback or benchmark drift data exists as explicit evidence (ref: ADR-032)
- Before moving prohibitions inside `directives[]` → stop and create a new ADR amending ADR-032 instead (ref: ADR-032)
- Before adding a new `gov` subcommand → verify it falls under the ADR-029 Rule 3 `gov <subcommand>` group permit, not a new top-level verb (ref: ADR-033)
- Before migrating a Python heredoc from tier-1 to tier-2 → verify the new Go subcommand is covered by the no-llm-in-tier-2 CI grep gate (ref: ADR-033)
- Before adding a tier-1 invocation of a `gov` subcommand → verify the markdown file declares `tier_2_dependency: edikt` and the call uses exit code only, never parses output shape (ref: ADR-033)
- Before adding a new command file → verify it lives in `commands/` as a `.md` file with no compiled code or build step (ref: INV-001)
- Before adding a hook script → verify it is under `templates/hooks/` only, is POSIX bash, and uses only stdlib Python (ref: INV-001)
- Before referencing a tier-2 binary from a tier-1 command → verify the command does not runtime-depend on the binary per ADR-015 (ref: INV-001)
- Before editing an accepted ADR → stop and create a new ADR that supersedes it instead (ref: INV-002)
- Before writing a superseding ADR → verify it references the old ADR with `Supersedes: ADR-NNN` and the old ADR's Status field is updated (ref: INV-002)
- Before writing a new hook script → verify JSON emission uses python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.dumps(...))' with untrusted values in sys.argv, never echo/printf string concatenation (ref: INV-003)
- Before merging changes to templates/hooks/*.sh or install.sh → verify CI lint grep returns zero matches for shell-concatenated JSON patterns (ref: INV-003)
- Before modifying any hook → verify the hook does not embed subagent-derived or file-content-derived text in systemMessage or additionalContext (ref: INV-004)
- Before adding hook CI lint rules → verify the rule rejects both the regex `(systemMessage|additionalContext)[^}]*\$\{` and bash code fences inside hook-protocol fields (ref: INV-004)
- Before editing any file under a governance-watched path → verify the edit does not overlap a sentinel-bounded managed region by byte-range check (ref: INV-005)
- Before writing to settings.json → verify ~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json is present and its managed-key hash matches the live file (ref: INV-005)
- Before running gov:compile after a v0.5.0 upgrade → verify every sentinel region gains a hash anchor on the first compile run (ref: INV-005)
- Before adding any argv/URL/prompt interpolation → verify the input is validated against an allowlist regex and passed as a separate argv element, not concatenated into a string (ref: INV-006)
- Before modifying phase-end-detector.sh or any hook using EVAL_MODEL or PLAN_STEM → verify each value has a validation gate before it reaches claude -p (ref: INV-006)
- Before writing a benchmark scorer → verify file_path scoring uses NFKC + casefold + strip before extension extraction (ref: INV-006)
- Before running tests → verify sandbox settings.json contains no hooks key and setting_sources excludes user (ref: INV-007)
- Before merging benchmark result directories → grep for credential patterns as enforced by the runner (ref: INV-007)
- Before writing any subprocess Claude invocation in tests → confirm setting_sources=["project"] only (ref: INV-007)
- Before updating the README install command → verify the URL pins to a specific tag, not main/ or releases/latest/download/ (ref: INV-008)
- Before cutting a release tag → verify pre-release CI grep returns zero branch-tracking URL matches in README.md, docs/, .github/workflows/ (ref: INV-008)
- Before composing install or upgrade URLs in bin/edikt → verify the tag is resolved and recorded as a specific tag, not a floating ref (ref: INV-008)
[edikt:reminders:end]: #

## Verification Checklist

Before finishing, verify each item. If any fails, fix before submitting.

- [ ] `/edikt:doctor` reports custom vs default status for templates, agents, and rules (ref: ADR-005)
- [ ] `/edikt:upgrade` never overwrites a file marked with `<!-- edikt:custom -->` or listed in `agents.custom` (ref: ADR-005)
- [ ] Extension files are concatenated correctly — test with a rule that has both base and extension (ref: ADR-005)
- [ ] grep BLOCKED templates/agents/evaluator.md — file contains BLOCKED in output format and execution-required rule (ref: ADR-010)
- [ ] grep BLOCKED templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md — file contains BLOCKED in output format (ref: ADR-010)
- [ ] grep for try-headless-then-fallback warning banner string in commands/sdlc/plan.md (ref: ADR-010)
- [ ] grep for evaluator probe invocation (claude -p) in commands/doctor.md (ref: ADR-010)
- [ ] Any new evaluator-invocation site reviewed — direct Agent() calls must be justified and must include BLOCKED-verdict contract (ref: ADR-010)
- [ ] EDIKT_SKIP_HOOK_TESTS does not appear as a required env var in test/run.sh (ref: ADR-011)
- [ ] All 9 hook test suites pass without EDIKT_SKIP_HOOK_TESTS=1 set: for t in test/unit/hooks/test_*.sh; do bash "$t"; done (ref: ADR-011)
- [ ] Every removed fixture pair has a _note and target_phase or target_contract in fixtures.yaml §9.1 (ref: ADR-011)
- [ ] _runner.sh does not contain run_staged_fixture — grep -q run_staged_fixture test/unit/hooks/_runner.sh should exit non-zero (ref: ADR-011)
- [ ] python3 -m pytest test/integration/regression/ --no-header -q passes without any environment variables set (ref: ADR-012)
- [ ] python3 -m pytest test/integration/test_init_greenfield.py --collect-only exits with 'claude CLI not authenticated' when neither ~/.claude/credentials nor ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is present (ref: ADR-012)
- [ ] Both ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and pytest.exit appear in test/integration/conftest.py (ref: ADR-012)
- [ ] CI workflow uses either ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret or a persisted claude session and both paths are documented (ref: ADR-012)
- [ ] test/integration/release/test_tarball_contents.sh verifies SHA256SUMS contains both tarball filenames and each hash is 64 hex characters (ref: ADR-013)
- [ ] bin/edikt install fetches and verifies against SHA256SUMS via existing verify_against_sidecar path (ref: ADR-013)
- [ ] .github/workflows/release.yml uploads SHA256SUMS as a release asset alongside the two tarballs (ref: ADR-013)
- [ ] All stdout-producing hooks (pre-tool-use, session-start, post-compact, subagent-stop, user-prompt-submit) produce valid JSON — run each through python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.loads(sys.stdin.read())' (ref: ADR-014)
- [ ] fixtures.yaml §9.1 documents resolution of the 3 removed pairs: pre-compact permanently removed; session-start-with-edikt and subagent-stop-critical deferred with explicit blocker notes (ref: ADR-014)
- [ ] Hook test suite passes without EDIKT_SKIP_HOOK_TESTS=1 — for t in test/unit/hooks/test_*.sh; do bash "$t"; done (ref: ADR-014)
- [ ] No user-facing message content changes between pre- and post-migration fixtures — git log -p test/expected/hook-outputs/ shows only JSON wrapping diffs (ref: ADR-014)
- [ ] ADR-011 status reads 'Superseded by ADR-014' in both frontmatter and bolded Status line (ref: ADR-014)
- [ ] `install.sh` contains no package-manager invocations and references only tier-1 artifacts (ref: ADR-015)
- [ ] `bin/edikt install benchmark` is the only `pip`-invoking code in the repo (ref: ADR-015)
- [ ] Every command file in `commands/` with a package-dependent helper declares `tier: 2` in frontmatter (ref: ADR-015)
- [ ] `test/integration/test_install_tier2.py::test_tier1_checksums_unchanged` passes after any tier-2 install/uninstall (ref: ADR-015)
- [ ] `/edikt:doctor` surfaces the tier-2 install state (which tools are installed, which are not) (ref: ADR-015)
- [ ] .github/workflows/release.yml publishes edikt-payload-v<ver>.tar.gz, SHA256SUMS, and SHA256SUMS.sig.bundle as Release assets (ref: ADR-016)
- [ ] install.sh and bin/edikt install both invoke cosign verify-blob with the regex identity before extracting any artifact (ref: ADR-016)
- [ ] With a tampered SHA256SUMS, verification fails and installer aborts with exit code 3 (EX_CHECKSUM) (ref: ADR-016)
- [ ] README install command uses releases/download/v<PINNED>/install.sh — grep -rn raw.githubusercontent.com/.*/main/ README.md docs/ returns zero matches (ref: ADR-016)
- [ ] ADR-013.md Status line reads Superseded by ADR-016 in the bolded Status line and frontmatter (ref: ADR-016)
- [ ] Fresh install produces a settings.json that validates against Claude Code's settings schema (ref: ADR-017)
- [ ] `cat ~/.claude/settings.json | jq .permissions.deny` lists every denied pattern from ADR-017 (ref: ADR-017)
- [ ] Running `curl http://example.com` under a fresh install produces a permission prompt, not silent allow (ref: ADR-017)
- [ ] Running `./test/run.sh` under a fresh install runs without a permission prompt (ref: ADR-017)
- [ ] `~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json` exists after install and records the canonical hash of the managed keys (ref: ADR-017)
- [ ] `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json` exists and validates against draft 2020-12 JSON Schema (ref: ADR-018)
- [ ] `templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md` and `templates/agents/evaluator.md` instruct the agent to emit conforming JSON (ref: ADR-018)
- [ ] `test/security/evaluator/test_evidence_gate.sh` passes: pytest criterion with evidence_type test_run → PASS preserved; pytest criterion with evidence_type grep → forced to BLOCKED; grandfathered verdict → evidence gate bypassed (ref: ADR-018)
- [ ] `bin/edikt upgrade` on a v0.4.3 snapshot emits the grandfather banner with a non-zero count (ref: ADR-018)
- [ ] templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh, file-changed.sh, headless-ask.sh, stop-failure.sh have been rewritten with fresh characterization fixtures per ADR-011's regen rule (ref: ADR-019)
- [ ] No other hook under templates/hooks/ has a content change bundled with its JSON-wrapping migration — verify via git log -p templates/hooks/*.sh on the branch (ref: ADR-019)
- [ ] Both ADR-014 and ADR-019 remain status: accepted with neither superseding the other (ref: ADR-019)
- [ ] tools/edikt/compile.py exists and installs successfully via `edikt install gov-compile` (ref: ADR-020)
- [ ] commands/gov/compile.md delegates deterministic steps to the tier-2 helper and does not contain 500+ lines of inline YAML/hash/render procedure (ref: ADR-020)
- [ ] Byte-equal output: two consecutive compiles of the same source produce byte-equal .claude/rules/ trees — verified by diff -r in CI (ref: ADR-020)
- [ ] No-op recompile (all hashes match) exits in under 500 ms with zero LLM calls (ref: ADR-020)
- [ ] /edikt:gov:compile --check runs in under 2 s on 40 source documents (ref: ADR-020)
- [ ] `tools/edikt/` contains `go.mod` and `go.sum` committed to the repo (ref: ADR-021)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` cross-compiles for darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, linux-amd64, linux-arm64 and uploads binaries as release assets (ref: ADR-021)
- [ ] `bin/edikt install gov-compile` verifies against cosign-signed `SHA256SUMS` before symlinking (ref: ADR-021)
- [ ] `install.sh` is POSIX bash with no Go binary dependency (ref: ADR-021)
- [ ] Any future tier-2 helper under `tools/<name>/` is a Go module following the same cross-compile and install contract (ref: ADR-021)
- [ ] `edikt version` runs natively in Go with no edikt-shell invocation confirmed (ref: ADR-022)
- [ ] `edikt gov compile [root]` completes in <= 20ms natively in Go with no edikt-shell invocation (ref: ADR-022)
- [ ] `edikt upgrade` execs edikt-shell with full stdio passthrough — output is byte-identical to running the bash launcher directly (ref: ADR-022)
- [ ] Removing bin/edikt-shell causes Go binary to emit a clear error and exit non-zero, not hang or panic (ref: ADR-022)
- [ ] grep for edikt-shell in compiled user-facing help output and error messages returns zero matches (ref: ADR-022)
- [ ] templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json exists and validates against JSON Schema draft 2020-12 (ref: ADR-025)
- [ ] templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md and templates/agents/evaluator.md contain no literal verdict path strings (ref: ADR-025)
- [ ] test/security/evaluator/test_evidence_gate.sh passes all three fixture cases: test_run PASS preserved, grep forced to BLOCKED, grandfathered bypassed (ref: ADR-025)
- [ ] bin/edikt upgrade on a v0.4.3 snapshot writes verdicts under the resolved paths.plans value, not a hardcoded path (ref: ADR-025)
- [ ] grep -rn 'docs/product/plans/verdicts' templates/ commands/ tools/ returns no matches outside ADR-018 and CHANGELOG (ref: ADR-025)
- [ ] `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` contains no keyword-grep agent identity block — grep for `INPUT_LOWER` and agent keyword patterns returns zero matches (ref: ADR-026)
- [ ] `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` contains no keyword-grep severity detection — grep for severity term patterns returns zero matches (ref: ADR-026)
- [ ] `test/fixtures/hook-payloads/subagent-stop-no-agent.json` exists with no `evaluator_output` and no canonical agent fields but content mentioning `architect` and `critical` (ref: ADR-026)
- [ ] `test/unit/hooks/test_subagent_stop.sh` includes the no-agent fixture in its FIXTURES array and passes (ref: ADR-026)
- [ ] `AGENT_IDENTITY_SOURCE` in `templates/hooks/subagent-stop.sh` takes only `evaluator_output` or `payload` — grep for `grep-fallback` returns zero matches (ref: ADR-026)
- [ ] `templates/schemas/sidecar.schema.json` exists and forbids `source_hash`, `agent_prompt_version`, `directives_hash` at root via `additionalProperties: false` (ref: ADR-027)
- [ ] `docs/architecture/decisions/ADR-008-*.md` has `status: superseded`, `superseded_by: ADR-027`, and `**Status:** Superseded by ADR-027` as its top body line (ref: ADR-027)
- [ ] `docs/architecture/decisions/ADR-027-*.md` exists with `status: accepted` and contains all required sections (ref: ADR-027)
- [ ] `gov:compile` re-run picks up ADR-027 directives and drops ADR-008 directives — verify ADR-027 directives appear in compiled topic file (ref: ADR-027)
- [ ] bin/edikt gov compile (without --check) runs Phase A on stale sidecars with concurrency 8, emits progress lines, then runs Phase B unconditionally on Phase A success (ref: ADR-028)
- [ ] bin/edikt gov compile --check exits 1 immediately when any sidecar is stale and emits the canonical actionable error directing the user to run edikt gov compile (ref: ADR-028)
- [ ] Static-analysis test in test/integration/governance/test_phase_b_purity.go verifies Phase B code path has no transitive reference to Agent/Task symbols (ref: ADR-028)
- [ ] ADR-020 frontmatter has amended_by: ADR-028 and its Status line reads Amended by ADR-028 (ref: ADR-028)
- [ ] Phase B satisfies <5s / <500ms / <2s SLOs on the 50-sidecar baseline corpus in test/perf/ (ref: ADR-028)
- [ ] `commands/sdlc/plan.md` declares `tier_2_dependency: edikt verify` and an `on_absent:` value in its frontmatter (ref: ADR-029)
- [ ] ADR-015's frontmatter contains `amended_by: ADR-029` and its `**Status:**` line reads `Accepted (Amended by ADR-029)` (ref: ADR-029)
- [ ] Integration test `test/integration/plan-tier2-absent.sh` (or equivalent) exercises the absence path with `bin/edikt` removed from PATH (ref: ADR-029)
- [ ] Tier-1 markdown commands that invoke tier-2 binaries restrict their calls to the eight enumerated verbs in Rule 3 (ref: ADR-029)
- [ ] `bin/edikt sidecar add-manual-directive --help` exits 0 (ref: ADR-031)
- [ ] `/edikt:adr:enrich` frontmatter declares `tier_2_dependency: edikt` and `on_absent: refuse-and-direct-user` (ref: ADR-031)
- [ ] `go vet ./cmd/...` clean (ref: ADR-031)
- [ ] All `TestAddManual_*` tests pass (ref: ADR-031)
- [ ] `templates/schemas/sidecar.v1.schema.json` declares `prohibitions[]` as an optional top-level array of objects (ref: ADR-032)
- [ ] `tools/edikt/internal/sidecar/sidecar.go` exposes `Prohibitions []Prohibition` as a top-level struct field (ref: ADR-032)
- [ ] `tools/edikt/internal/sidecardiff/sidecardiff.go` references `sc.Prohibitions` directly without filter logic (ref: ADR-032)
- [ ] `tools/edikt/internal/phaseb/merge.go` emits a separate `[edikt:prohibitions:start/end]` managed region (ref: ADR-032)
- [ ] `tools/edikt/internal/benchmark/benchmark.go` walks `sc.Prohibitions` to generate per-rejection attack entries (ref: ADR-032)
- [ ] `bin/edikt gov compile-history --help` exits 0 (ref: ADR-033)
- [ ] `bin/edikt gov gitignore-bootstrap --help` exits 0 (ref: ADR-033)
- [ ] `bin/edikt gov directive-check --help` exits 0 (ref: ADR-033)
- [ ] `commands/gov/compile.md`, `commands/gov/_shared-directive-checks.md`, and `commands/doctor.md` contain no `python3 - <<` heredocs (ref: ADR-033)
- [ ] CI grep gate in `.github/workflows/sidecar-checks.yml` covers `tools/edikt/cmd/gov/compilehistory.go`, `cmd/gov/gitignorebootstrap.go`, `cmd/gov/directivecheck.go`, `internal/dircheck/`, `internal/gitignore/`, `internal/orphan/` (ref: ADR-033)
- [ ] No `package.json` or root-level `go.mod` exists at the tier-1 root — check repo root (ref: INV-001)
- [ ] All files in `commands/` have `.md` extension — grep for non-.md files (ref: INV-001)
- [ ] All `.sh` files in `templates/` are under `templates/hooks/` only — find templates/ -name '*.sh' -not -path '*/hooks/*' (ref: INV-001)
- [ ] No tier-2 binaries committed to repo — check `tools/` for compiled binaries (ref: INV-001)
- [ ] No accepted ADR has had its content edited — only Status field may read `Superseded by ADR-NNN` (ref: INV-002)
- [ ] Any new ADR that supersedes an older one includes `Supersedes: ADR-NNN` in its frontmatter or body (ref: INV-002)
- [ ] Superseded ADRs have Status updated to `Superseded by ADR-NNN` and no other content changes (ref: INV-002)
- [ ] grep for echo/printf JSON shell concatenation in templates/hooks/*.sh and install.sh returns zero matches (ref: INV-003)
- [ ] test/unit/hooks/test_*.sh all pass with inputs containing embedded quotes, backslashes, and newlines (ref: INV-003)
- [ ] No hook script uses heredoc or printf for JSON construction in templates/hooks/ (ref: INV-003)
- [ ] CI lint rejects hooks whose output matches `(systemMessage|additionalContext)[^}]*\$\{` (ref: INV-004)
- [ ] CI lint rejects hooks whose output contains a markdown code fence labelled bash inside a hook-protocol field (ref: INV-004)
- [ ] Security regression test exists in test/security/hooks/ that injects `'; rm /tmp/sentinel; '` and asserts /tmp/sentinel is never removed (ref: INV-004)
- [ ] Pre-tool-use hook performs byte-range overlap check — not regex on old_string/new_string — for edits under governance-watched paths (ref: INV-005)
- [ ] ~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json is present and records managed-key hash for any edikt-managed settings.json (ref: INV-005)
- [ ] test/security/sentinel/ tests cover adjacent, inside, outside, cross-boundary, CRLF, Unicode, hash mismatch, and bootstrap cases (ref: INV-005)
- [ ] Managed regions without a hash anchor are blocked by byte-range and seeded by compile, not skipped (ref: INV-005)
- [ ] Unit tests under test/security/inputs/ cover every validator with positive, negative, Unicode-variant, trailing-whitespace, null-byte, and directory-traversal cases (ref: INV-006)
- [ ] CI lint flags any grep match of $(PLAN_STEM|EVAL_MODEL|REF_TAG|EDIKT_HOOK_DIR) in shell scripts not preceded by a validation gate (ref: INV-006)
- [ ] /edikt:sdlc:audit checks this invariant for any new argv/URL/prompt interpolation (ref: INV-006)
- [ ] Security regression tests under test/security/sandbox/ pass and assert sandbox settings.json has no hooks key (ref: INV-007)
- [ ] CI grep: grep -rn 'setting_sources=\[.*user' test/ returns zero matches (ref: INV-007)
- [ ] test/integration/benchmarks/results/claude-*/ is present in .gitignore (ref: INV-007)
- [ ] Benchmark run does not copy host ~/.claude/settings.json into sandbox (ref: INV-007)
- [ ] Result JSONL redacts tool_calls content and aborts on credential-pattern detection (ref: INV-007)
- [ ] grep -rnE '(raw\.githubusercontent\.com/.+/main/|releases/latest/download/)' README.md docs/ .github/workflows/ returns zero matches (ref: INV-008)
- [ ] Security regression test under test/security/release/ passes with no branch-tracking URL detected in user-facing files (ref: INV-008)
- [ ] resolve_latest_tag result is recorded as a specific tag in installer receipt or ~/.edikt/state/version (ref: INV-008)

## Reminder: Non-Negotiable Constraints

These constraints were listed above and are restated for emphasis.
Do not violate them under any circumstances.

- Every edikt tier-1 command MUST be a `.md` file, and every tier-1 template MUST be `.md`, `.yaml`, or `.sh`. No TypeScript, no compiled binaries, no build step in tier-1. (ref: INV-001)
- The tier-1 system MUST work by copying files into `~/.claude/commands/`. (ref: INV-001)
- `.sh` MUST only be used for hook templates under `templates/hooks/` — those scripts MUST be POSIX bash with stdlib-only Python heredocs (no third-party runtime deps). (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 files MUST NOT runtime-depend on tier-2 binaries. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 MUST NOT have a `package.json`, root-level `go.mod`, or build files at the tier-1 root. (ref: INV-001)
- All tier-1 commands MUST live in `commands/` as `.md` files. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-1 templates MUST be plain `.md`, `.yaml`, or (under `templates/hooks/` only) `.sh` files in `templates/`. `.sh` hook templates MUST be POSIX bash and MUST NOT depend on third-party runtimes — embedded Python heredocs are stdlib-only. (ref: INV-001)
- Tier-2 code MUST live under `tools/<name>/` with its own `go.mod`; tier-2 binaries MUST NOT be committed to the repo. (ref: INV-001)
- Architecture Decision Records MUST be immutable once accepted. (ref: INV-002)
- Once the status is changed to `accepted`, its content — context, decision, and consequences — MUST never be edited again. (ref: INV-002)
- The only permitted mutation after acceptance is updating the `Status:` field to `Superseded by ADR-NNN` or `Deprecated`. (ref: INV-002)
- When a decision changes, a new ADR MUST be written that supersedes the old one. (ref: INV-002)
- The old ADR MUST be updated to `Status: Superseded by ADR-NNN` and left otherwise intact. (ref: INV-002)
- When asked to update, fix, or improve an accepted ADR: stop and create a new ADR instead. (ref: INV-002)
- The new ADR must reference the superseded one (`Supersedes: ADR-NNN`). (ref: INV-002)
- The old ADR status line must be updated to `Superseded by ADR-NNN`. (ref: INV-002)
- No other changes to the accepted ADR are permitted. (ref: INV-002)
- Every hook that emits JSON MUST construct that JSON via a structured serializer with untrusted values passed as data (not concatenated into a string). (ref: INV-003)
- Building JSON by shell string concatenation is NEVER permitted in hooks. (ref: INV-003)
- Pre-merge CI lint MUST grep for echo or printf JSON shell-concatenation patterns in templates/hooks/*.sh and install.sh and MUST return zero matches. (ref: INV-003)
- Unit-test fixtures per hook MUST assert output is `json.loads`-parseable when inputs contain embedded `"`, `\`, and newline characters. (ref: INV-003)
- `/edikt:sdlc:audit` and `/edikt:sdlc:review` MUST read INV-003 before reviewing any change under `templates/hooks/`. (ref: INV-003)
- Hooks MUST NOT emit a `systemMessage`, `additionalContext`, or other Claude-facing channel whose body contains a shell command interpolating subagent-derived, file-content-derived, git-identity-derived, or otherwise attacker-influenceable text. (ref: INV-004)
- Hooks that need to record state (event logs, gate firings, override decisions) MUST perform that recording themselves from within the hook process. (ref: INV-004)
- Claude MUST receive only static instructions plus structured data. (ref: INV-004)
- CI lint MUST reject any hook whose output contains the regex `(systemMessage|additionalContext)[^}]*\$\{` OR whose output contains a markdown code fence labelled `bash` inside a hook-protocol field. (ref: INV-004)
- Security regression tests under `test/security/hooks/` MUST include at least one test per hook that attempts to inject `'; rm /tmp/sentinel; '` into an agent-derived field and asserts the file `/tmp/sentinel` is never removed. (ref: INV-004)
- Every edikt-managed region in a user file has a cryptographic integrity mechanism that MUST be verified before the region is overwritten. (ref: INV-005)
- Managed markdown regions MUST be validated by byte-range overlap of the resolved file — NEVER by regex over `old_string` or `new_string`. (ref: INV-005)
- The install/upgrade writer MUST verify the live JSON's managed-key hash against the sidecar before overwriting managed keys. (ref: INV-005)
- An edit whose resolved byte range (markdown) or managed-key hash (JSON) would mutate a managed region MUST be blocked unless issued by an explicitly allowlisted edikt operation (compile, install, upgrade, migration). (ref: INV-005)
- The pre-tool-use hook MUST execute the byte-range guard on every Edit/Write under governance-watched paths. (ref: INV-005)
- Every managed sentinel region MUST carry a hash anchor after the first compile following v0.5.0 upgrade; missing anchor on an active block MUST be seeded by compile. (ref: INV-005)
- Unit tests at `test/security/sentinel/` MUST cover the overlap matrix (adjacent, inside, outside, cross-boundary, CRLF, Unicode) plus the hash mismatch and bootstrap paths. (ref: INV-005)
- `~/.edikt/state/settings-managed.json` is required for any edikt-managed `settings.json`; its absence MUST trigger the interactive permissions prompt on upgrade. (ref: INV-005)
- Any value that originates outside edikt's immediate control — filenames discovered via filesystem scan, configuration values read from `.edikt/config.yaml`, environment variables, CLI flags (`--ref`, `--model`, etc.), URL refs, and hook payload fields — MUST be validated against an allowlist regex before it is interpolated into a shell argv element, a URL, a prompt string, or a file path. (ref: INV-006)
- Validators MUST normalize with NFKC + casefold + whitespace-strip so that Unicode lookalikes and trailing whitespace cannot bypass allowlist comparisons. (ref: INV-006)
- Attacker-influenceable values that flow into argv MUST be passed as separate argv elements, never interpolated into a quoted string that is then evaluated. (ref: INV-006)
- Sandboxes created by the test harness or benchmark runner to execute Claude sessions MUST be hermetic. (ref: INV-007)
- Test and benchmark sandboxes MUST NOT copy the host user's `~/.claude/settings.json`, user-global settings, hooks, shell rc files, SSH keys, or any other host credential or configuration into the sandbox. (ref: INV-007)
- The `setting_sources` list passed to subprocess Claude invocations in tests MUST be restricted to `["project"]`; `"user"` is forbidden. (ref: INV-007)
- The sandbox MUST receive only a curated minimal `settings.json` written by the test harness. (ref: INV-007)
- Security regression tests under `test/security/sandbox/` MUST assert that, for a representative benchmark run, the sandbox `settings.json` contains no `hooks` key and no entries outside the curated allowlist. (ref: INV-007)
- CI grep: `grep -rn 'setting_sources=\[.*user' test/` MUST return zero matches. (ref: INV-007)
- `test/integration/benchmarks/results/claude-*/` MUST be `.gitignore`d; committed result directories (summaries/baselines) MUST be grepped for credential patterns pre-merge. (ref: INV-007)
- Every user-facing install, upgrade, or documentation URL that resolves to a mutable source — raw.githubusercontent.com, releases/latest/download/, branch refs, or any path a repo owner can silently update — MUST NOT be used. (ref: INV-008)
- All user-facing URLs MUST pin to a specific release tag (releases/download/vX.Y.Z/... or an equivalent immutable ref). (ref: INV-008)
- Pre-release CI check MUST grep README.md docs/ .github/workflows/ and return zero matches for raw.githubusercontent.com/.+/main/ or releases/latest/download/ before a release tag is cut. (ref: INV-008)
- Security regression test under test/security/release/ MUST assert no branch-tracking URL is present in user-facing files. (ref: INV-008)
- Release runbook MUST include a step to update the pinned version in README and re-run the grep. (ref: INV-008)
